title: A vulnerability was detected in antlr ANTLR4 up to 4.13.2. Affected by this issue is the function...
description: A vulnerability was detected in antlr ANTLR4 up to 4.13.2. Affected by this issue is the function getImportedVocabFile of the file tool/src/org/antlr/v4/parse/TokenVocabParser.java of the component tokenVocab Grammar Option Handler. The manipulation results in path traversal. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in ANTLR4 up to version 4.13.2 allows for path traversal attacks via the getImportedVocabFile function. This could enable attackers to access unauthorized files or directories on the server, potentially exposing sensitive data or enabling further exploitation.
recommended_actions: Upgrade ANTLR4 to the latest version once a patch is released to address CVE-2026-13503.
Implement input validation and sanitization to mitigate path traversal risks in the affected component.
Monitor systems for unusual activity and apply file access restrictions to sensitive directories.

title: A security vulnerability has been detected in antlr ANTLR4 up to 4.13.2. Affected by this...
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in antlr ANTLR4 up to 4.13.2. Affected by this vulnerability is the function GoTarget of the file tool/src/org/antlr/v4/codegen/target/GoTarget.java of the component gofmt. The manipulation leads to command injection. The attack can only be performed from a local environment. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in ANTLR4 up to version 4.13.2 allows for command injection via the GoTarget function in the gofmt component. However, exploitation requires local access, reducing the likelihood of widespread attacks. Public disclosure increases the risk of targeted exploitation.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to a patched version of ANTLR4 if available, or apply mitigations to restrict local access to vulnerable systems.
Monitor systems for unusual activity or unauthorized local access that may indicate exploitation attempts.
Engage with the vendor to confirm if a patch or workaround will be provided and implement additional security controls in the meantime.

title: A weakness has been identified in antlr ANTLR4 up to 4.13.2. Affected is an unknown function of...
description: A weakness has been identified in antlr ANTLR4 up to 4.13.2. Affected is an unknown function of the file tool/src/org/antlr/v4/codegen/model/OutputFile.java of the component Grammar Action Block Handler. Executing a manipulation can lead to code injection. The attack may be launched remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The identified weakness in ANTLR4 up to version 4.13.2 allows for remote code injection via a vulnerability in the Grammar Action Block Handler. If exploited, attackers could execute arbitrary code on affected systems, potentially compromising data integrity, confidentiality, and system availability.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade to a patched version of ANTLR4 (if available) or apply any available mitigations to prevent exploitation.
Review and monitor systems using ANTLR4 for any signs of unauthorized access or suspicious activity, particularly in components handling grammar parsing.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization measures to reduce the risk of code injection attacks.

title: A flaw has been found in AIDC-AI ComfyUI-Copilot up to 2.0.28. This issue affects some unknown...
description: A flaw has been found in AIDC-AI ComfyUI-Copilot up to 2.0.28. This issue affects some unknown processing of the file backend/controller/conversation_api.py of the component Workflow Checkpoint Restore Handler. Executing a manipulation can lead to improper control of resource identifiers. The attack may be performed from remote. A high complexity level is associated with this attack. The exploitability is assessed as difficult. The exploit has been published and may be used. The pull request to

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in AIDC-AI ComfyUI-Copilot up to version 2.0.28 allows improper control of resource identifiers in the Workflow Checkpoint Restore Handler. While the attack requires high complexity and is difficult to exploit, the existence of a published exploit increases the risk of targeted attacks. Potential impacts include unauthorized access or manipulation of workflow resources.
recommended_actions: Immediately update AIDC-AI ComfyUI-Copilot to a version beyond 2.0.28 if available, or apply any patches provided by the vendor.
Restrict access to the affected component (backend/controller/conversation_api.py) by implementing network-level controls such as firewalls or VPNs.
Monitor systems for unusual activity, especially related to resource identifiers, and apply intrusion detection systems to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title: A security vulnerability has been detected in glpi-project glpi 11.0.5/11.0.6/11.0.7. This...
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in glpi-project glpi 11.0.5/11.0.6/11.0.7. This affects the function Document::canViewFile of the file front/document.send.php of the component Document Handler. Such manipulation of the argument docid leads to authorization bypass. The attack can be executed remotely. This attack is characterized by high complexity. It is indicated that the exploitability is difficult. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Document Handler of glpi-project glpi versions 11.0.5, 11.0.6, and 11.0.7 could allow unauthorized users to bypass authorization checks and access restricted files. This could lead to unauthorized data exposure or potential data breaches.
recommended_actions: Immediately update glpi-project glpi to the latest patched version if available, or apply any vendor-recommended mitigations.
Review and restrict access to the affected component (front/document.send.php) to minimize exposure until the vulnerability is resolved.
Conduct a thorough audit of access logs to identify any unauthorized access attempts or suspicious activities related to the 'docid' parameter.

title:
CVE-2026-52911 - In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

ksmbd: scope conn->binding slow
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

ksmbd: scope conn->binding slowpath to bound sessions only

When the binding SESSION_SETUP sets conn->binding = true, the flag stays
set after the call so that the global session lookup in
ksmbd_session_lookup_all() can find the session, which was not added to
conn->sessions. Because the flag is connection-wide, the global lookup
path will also resolve any other session by id if asked.

Tighten the global lookup so that the returned session must have this
connection registered in its channel xarray (sess->ksmbd_chann_list).
The channel entry is installed by the existing binding_session path in
ntlm_authenticate()/krb5_authenticate() when a SESSION_SETUP completes
successfully, so this condition is a strict equivalent of "this
connection has been accepted as a channel of this session". Connections
that have not bound to a given session cannot reach it via the global
table.

The existing conn->binding gate for entering the slowpath is preserved
so that non-binding connections keep the fast-path-only behavior, and
the session->state check is unchanged.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-52911.
